name: Cross-tool threading validation
summary: Validate the current multithreading option contract across implemented pairs-rs tools without making performance claims.
goal: Add cross-tool tests proving deterministic output for implemented threaded paths, BGZF validity, all-Rust pipeline thread propagation, and loud failures for unsupported threaded I/O options.
explicit_non_goals: Do not implement new threaded I/O behavior.
Do not change parse, sort, merge, dedup, select, split, or stats runtime behavior.
Do not claim speedup or full pairtools parity.
oracle_commands: Not applicable; M162 validates pairs-rs thread-option behavior and deterministic equivalence across thread counts.
acceptance_criteria: Sort output is identical after decompression for `--nproc 1` and a higher `--nproc` on a fixture large enough to exercise chunk sorting.
Stats output is identical after decompression for single-threaded and threaded BGZF input/output settings.
The all-Rust pipeline dry-run propagates `SORT_THREADS` to pairs-rs sort and samtools commands.
Parse, merge, dedup, select, and split threaded options that are not implemented fail loudly instead of being silently accepted.
No benchmark or speedup claim is made.
known_risks: The test validates thread-option contracts and deterministic outputs, not CPU utilization or speedup.
Some implemented commands still intentionally reject `--nproc-in` and `--nproc-out`; this milestone records that contract instead of expanding behavior.
